goal: Automated multi-perspective code review system for a React/TypeScript web application. Each agent reviews the same code change from a different lens, then results are synthesized into a unified report.
description: A multi-agent code review system where specialist agents perform parallel reviews of the same code change. Each agent has a distinct focus area: general code quality (correctness, readability, architecture), security vulnerabilities, test coverage and strategy, and web performance. The system supports both single-agent and parallel fan-out modes, with slash commands orchestrating the workflow.
tasks: Review code changes for correctness, readability, and architecture quality
Audit code for security vulnerabilities and threat modeling
Analyze test coverage and design test suites
Audit web performance (Core Web Vitals, loading, rendering)
Synthesize findings from multiple agents into unified report
Handle parallel fan-out reviews across all 4 specialists
Support standing review for specific dimensions on demand
safety_requirements: No agent delegates to another agent — recommendations only
Each agent operates independently from its own perspective
Slash commands orchestrate fan-out, not agent-to-agent calls
Clear separation of concerns between agents
integration_requirements: Claude Code agent system (.md files in .claude/agents/)
Synthesized output in markdown report format
